nature.meaning: A tall plant with a trunk, branches, and leaves
nature.example: The big oak tree gives shade on sunny days.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Trees are tall plants that give us shade and air.

nature.meaning: A colorful plant part that makes seeds
nature.example: The yellow flower smells sweet and has soft petals.

nature.meaning: Green plants that cover the ground
nature.example: I run barefoot on the soft green grass.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Grass is the green carpet on the ground.

nature.meaning: A very high piece of land that goes up into the sky
nature.example: The mountain has snow on its top even in summer.

nature.meaning: A large natural flow of water
nature.example: The fish swim in the river and the water flows to the sea.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: A river is water that moves across land.

nature.meaning: A very large body of salt water
nature.example: The ocean is so big you cannot see the other side.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: The ocean is the big, salty water covering most of Earth.

nature.meaning: A white or gray fluffy thing in the sky made of water
nature.example: The cloud looks like a bunny floating in the sky.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Clouds are fluffy white things in the sky.

nature.meaning: Water that falls from clouds to the ground
nature.example: I use my umbrella when the rain falls on my head.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Rain is water that falls from the sky.

nature.meaning: White frozen water that falls like flakes in winter
nature.example: We build a snowman when the snow covers the ground.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Snow is white, fluffy cold stuff that falls in winter.

nature.meaning: The star that gives us light and heat
nature.example: The sun rises in the morning and sets at night.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: The sun is the big bright thing in the sky.

nature.meaning: The round object in the night sky that shines
nature.example: The moon looks like a banana when it is a crescent.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: The moon is the white circle in the night sky.

nature.meaning: A tiny bright dot in the night sky
nature.example: I see a million stars twinkling in the dark sky.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Stars are little lights in the night sky.

nature.meaning: A hard piece of earth or stone
nature.example: I collect smooth rocks from the beach.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Rocks are hard pieces of earth.

nature.meaning: Tiny grains of rock found on beaches
nature.example: I build a castle with wet sand at the beach.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Sand is tiny, soft grains on the beach.

nature.example: The sky is blue on a sunny day and gray when it rains.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: The sky is what you see when you look up.
